free_text: Abrupt Change in Radiation-Width Distribution for
147Sm Neutron Resonances
Experiment was performed using DANCE
on flight path 14 at the Manuel Lujan, Jr. Neutron
Scattering Center at LANSCE.
Metallic samarium, enriched to
97.93% in 147Sm, with diameter of 1 cm and weight of
10.410 mg was used. Sample was mounted on a thin Al
backing and placed in the center of DANCE array.
Experiment performed using 4Pi gamma ray array
DANCE consisting of 160 BaF2 crystals designed for
studying neutron capture cross sections on small
samples.
Energy of incident neutrons were determined by
time-of-flight method
Neutrons are generated at LANSCE via
spallation reactions when an 800-MeV proton beam
strikes a tungsten target. Ambient-temperature water
moderator was used. The resulting neutron flux peaks
near thermal energy and is approximately proportional
to 1/En over the range of measurements.
No information given
Compiled by S.H.
